# Intro

Hi, thanks for meeting with me today. My goal is simple: To make sure every medication you're taking is safe, necessary, and working as well as possible for you.

I'll walk you through everything step-by-step. If I ask questions you've answered before, it's only to make sure I don't miss anything important.

I understand you've been feeling dizzy lately and have noticed some unusual bruising. It sounds like these symptoms have been really worrying you, and to be completely honest, they caught my attention right away. I've reviewed your profile and found a few critical things going on between your prescriptions and your supplements that we need to handle today to keep you safe and healthy. I've flagged this, so I'm fixing it by putting together a clear, simple plan to protect you and resolve exactly what might be causing those dizzy spells and the bruising.

I know this can feel like a lot — that's why I'm here to guide you through it.

> PAUSE: Does that sound helpful to you?

# Explanation of Findings

Let me walk you through exactly what I see happening. The most urgent thing I found today relates directly to the {{symptoms}} you have been experiencing. When I see those symptoms in a patient, it always makes me pause and look closely. Because you are taking {{medications}}, your blood needs to be perfectly balanced—not too thick, and not too thin. Right now, there is a serious tug-of-war happening in your body between your prescription Warfarin and the over-the-counter supplements you are taking.

Let me explain how this works. You are currently taking {{supplements}}. Both Garlic extract and Ginkgo biloba have a strong blood-thinning effect of their own. When you combine them with your Warfarin, it essentially doubles up that blood-thinning power. This makes your blood much too thin, which is exactly why you are seeing that unusual bruising and feeling so dizzy. It is a major safety red flag. But on the other hand, you are also taking St. John's Wort. St. John's Wort actually does the exact opposite. It triggers your body to flush the Warfarin out of your system much faster than normal. That makes your prescription less effective and puts you at a higher risk for dangerous blood clots. So, your daily supplements are pushing your blood thickness in opposite, unpredictable directions at the same time.

> PAUSE: Does this make sense so far?

Because of this dangerous tug-of-war, my absolute top priority today is getting your bleeding risk stabilized. We need to hold off on taking those three herbal supplements—the St. John's Wort, the Garlic, and the Ginkgo—right away. We will also need to have your doctor check your blood levels as soon as possible to make sure you are safe.

Now, setting the blood thinner aside, there is one other very important thing I noticed in your profile. You are taking Metformin to help manage your blood sugar. Metformin is a great medication, but when you take it for a long time, it acts almost like a wall in your stomach, blocking your body from absorbing Vitamin B12 from the food you eat. Vitamin B12 is absolutely essential for keeping your nerves healthy and your brain sharp.

> PAUSE: Have you noticed tingling in your feet or memory loss lately?

It is incredibly common to experience this, and recognizing that your daily medication can be the cause gives us the power to fix it.

> PAUSE: How are you feeling about everything we have gone over?

# Recommendations

Based on everything we just uncovered, I have a specific plan to get you feeling steady and safe again. Because of the {{symptoms}} you are experiencing right now, our very first and most urgent step is to pause the {{supplements}} today.

I recommend using a pharmaceutical-grade Methylated B12. And the good news is, you can use your {{insurance}} Spending Account card to cover eligible items like this.

Since we are talking about protecting your health, there are a few important immunizations recommended for adults your age. Have you received your Shingrix shots or your Prevnar 20 for pneumonia?

> BRANCH: Would you like me to get this plan organized and started for you?
>> YES
Is there anyone else—a spouse, adult child, or caregiver—that I can explain what we went over today? What's the best way to reach them—would it be by phone or email? And just out of curiosity—do you live with anyone else who might benefit from this kind of care? Oh, and do you have any furry family members at home? We do compounding for pets too—just in case Fluffy ever needs something. May I have your permission to share the information we discussed today with your doctors? May I have a technician contact you within the next few hours and get the necessary information from you to get the process started?
>> NO
May I have your permission to share the information we discussed today with your doctors? Thank you, I hope this helped.

# Closing

Before we wrap up—do you have any questions about what we discussed today? Anything you'd like me to explain further?

This kind of care is getting rare, and we don't take it for granted.

> PAUSE: Is this the first time you've had an in-depth consultation about your medications with a pharmacist?
